title: salix rigida f. subintegra
intro: Salix rigida f. subintegra, also known as a dwarf form of the Russian willow (Salix rigida), is a shrub-like plant native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Salix rigida f. subintegra include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. The plant is commonly prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other herbal remedies.
